title: Ecosystem
subtitle: A broad Active Inference ecosystem spanning scientific domains, technical implementations, social systems, and applied fields.
audience: Visitors mapping domains, partners, projects, and application areas.
lede: The Active Inference ecosystem includes challenge areas, user segments, information architecture, organizations, projects, and domains of application.

sections.heading: Priorities and Challenge Areas
sections.body: Ecosystem development focuses on structure, growth, cyber and cognitive security, information flows, and organizations facing problems that may be addressed by Active Inference.

sections.heading: Domains of Application
sections.body: Active Inference connects with biology, neuroscience, mental health, bioregional modeling, category theory, computation, economics, education, philosophy, physics, robotics, legal systems, social systems, logistics, scientific method, and decentralized science.

sections.heading: Ecosystem Support
sections.body: Ecosystem support includes public communication, learning infrastructure, project visibility, partner pathways, research resources, and shared open-source tooling.

cards.title: Biology and Neuroscience
cards.text: Modeling living systems, cognition, behavior, and theoretical neurobiology.

cards.title: Mental Health
cards.text: Belief updating, clinical modeling, and therapeutic-relevant theory.

cards.title: Bioregional Modeling
cards.text: Ecological, agricultural, soil, and geospatial applications.

cards.title: Computation
cards.text: Software implementations, agent models, notations, and knowledge infrastructure.

cards.title: Social Systems
cards.text: Group cognition, institutions, coordination, and social science.

cards.title: Decentralized Science
cards.text: Epistemic commons and open collaboration structures.
